item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article could not be sourced. Thus, it is to be assumed that it's non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since no one said it: the mention of "run people over for money" is probably a reference to the computer game (surprise, surprise) Carmageddon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"…and the aircraft was hijacked by some radical traffic cabs."

How many people would you bet have ever said or had cause to say THAT before?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bizarre, at times funny, a little deep. This one is reeeaaally interesting. Love it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A society of vehicles… that have the capacity to travel through time. It is a lovely deconstruction of several films (Cars and Back to the Future) that I sincerely enjoy.

Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The initial bits could have used some better wording (I got very confused throughout the description), but the interviews were good. And I ended up with a legitimate reason to use the phrase "time-traveling taxi terrorists" so… upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This wasn't too bad. The uniqueness of the whole thing was what got me really liking it, though. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Finally got me to upvote this one.
The previously narmy OTT logs always made me downvote- playing them serious with just a little understated humor this time around makes all the difference.

One little thing does still bother the car guy in me: the odometer being stuck at 999,999. Because of the way they're geared, odometers don't "see" any difference between rolling from 999999 to 000000 and rolling from 599999 to 600000- there's no reset mechanism to break. Mechanically, getting stuck at something arbitrary like 67290[randomly flailing number] is much more likely than getting stuck at 999999. Might be better to say "my odometer rolled over from 999999 4 times before it broke" or something similar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I understand your sentiment. However, these cars have a purpose when they do a spacetime jump to arrive here, at Earth. Firstly, doing a jump in their world is illegal. Second, you also have to remember that these cars are wrecked when they're being interviewed, so they also risk that. Third, it is implied that cars are purposely trying to travel back to this time.

Aside from -12, all other cars interviewed have a motive to travel back. -17 is an outcast, -19 wants to see its mother, and the aircraft was hijacked by some radical traffic cabs.

If such a risk is taken to escape from one world to another, you would want to tell your story objectively.

As a final note, the interviews serve as our only indication towards what "car world" might be like. There's no way to travel to their world, so interviews are the next best thing.

I hope that clears up an issues. If anything else doesn't make sense, feel free to ask.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like the interviews are basically exposition. Phrases like "Now that I think of it, we have a strange society." don't feel genuine to me. If I were suddenly taken out of my element, I don't think I'd have such an objective opinions of my world. I think this would be more successful if we were able to see the cars' world not because it is explained to us, but because they are so a part of it that their actions and speech paint a picture for us. "I hate my peers. I hate this society." Those phrases ring false to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first, I couldn't tell what was different this time around, but I knew it was better. Then I saw your announcement.

Cars are devoid of malicious intent or excessive whining. Rather, they are lost souls who wish to restore former glory. They jump the portal to escape their hell.

Then I knew what changed, and how. That was the right direction to take with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one has an air of mystery to it. A little bit of tragedy too, I think. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is too wacko not to like.

All because I was caught inhaling bio-fuel as a teen.

Yeah. Upvoted.

item.title: Author Note
item.content:encoded:
Hey guys.

This is a remake of an older SCP I posted.

Thanks to weizhong, Zolgamax, Dr_Leonard, djkaktus, Choiwel, and everyone else who looked over this in chat.
